prompt:
While researching a peat bog excavation, a student took the following notes:
• The bog preserved a 4,000-year-old canoe.
• Low oxygen kept the wood from rotting.
• The find reveals ancient travel routes.

The student wants to explain why the canoe survived so long. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Buried deep in the peat, the canoe turned out to be roughly four thousand years old when experts examined it.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain why the canoe survived so long as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Because the bog's waterlogged, low-oxygen mud kept the wood from rotting, a canoe survived there for some 4,000 years.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to explain why the canoe survived so long.

choices.text: The well-preserved canoe pulled from the bog offers fresh clues about the routes ancient people once traveled.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain why the canoe survived so long as the prompt asks.

choices.text: A bog, a soft wetland built up from layers of decaying plants, forms slowly over many thousands of years.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain why the canoe survived so long as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to explain why the canoe survived so long; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a city composting program, a student took the following notes:
• Residents sort food scraps into green bins.
• The scraps become compost for public parks.
• The program cut landfill waste by a fifth.

The student wants to report the program's main result. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Across the city, residents now carefully sort their leftover food scraps into the green bins set out for collection.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not report the program's main result as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Composting, the natural breakdown of organic matter into rich soil, has long been practiced by home gardeners.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not report the program's main result as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Since residents began sorting food scraps into green bins, the city has cut the waste sent to its landfill by a fifth.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to report the program's main result.

choices.text: The compost made from the collected scraps is later spread across the soil of the city's many public parks.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not report the program's main result as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to report the program's main result; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a library renovation, a student took the following notes:
• The library added a quiet study wing.
• Weekly visits rose by a third.
• Evening hours were extended.

The student wants to report the effect of the changes. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: After the library added a quiet study wing and extended evening hours, weekly visits rose by a third.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to report the effect of the changes.

choices.text: Weekly visits to the library rose by a full third, a jump the staff were pleased to see after months of construction.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not report the effect of the changes as the prompt asks.

choices.text: A public library, which lends books and other materials at no charge, serves readers of every age in its community.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not report the effect of the changes as the prompt asks.

choices.text: As part of the renovation, the library added a brand-new quiet study wing and kept its doors open later into the evening.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not report the effect of the changes as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to report the effect of the changes; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a vertical farm, a student took the following notes:
• The farm grows lettuce in stacked indoor trays.
• It uses 95% less water than a field.
• It produces greens year-round in any weather.

The student wants to emphasize the water savings. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Vertical farming, a method of growing crops in stacked layers, is being tried in cities short on open land.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the water savings as the prompt asks.

choices.text: By growing lettuce in stacked indoor trays, the farm produces year-round greens while using 95% less water than a field.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to emphasize the water savings.

choices.text: The farm relies on stacked trays of lettuce arranged in tall rows inside a carefully controlled indoor space.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the water savings as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Because it operates entirely indoors, the vertical farm can produce fresh greens during every season of the year.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the water savings as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to emphasize the water savings; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a bicycle-sharing launch, a student took the following notes:
• The city placed 500 bikes at 60 stations.
• Riders took 12,000 trips in the first week.
• Most trips replaced short car rides.

The student wants to show that the program changed how people travel. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: In its first week, the 500-bike program logged 12,000 trips, most of them replacing short car rides.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to show that the program changed how people travel.

choices.text: In just its first week of operation, the new bike-sharing program recorded an impressive total of 12,000 separate trips.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not show that the program changed how people travel as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Bicycles, a common and affordable form of city transportation, are used by commuters and visitors alike in many places.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not show that the program changed how people travel as the prompt asks.

choices.text: To launch the program, the city carefully placed a fleet of 500 bicycles at 60 docking stations across the downtown area.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not show that the program changed how people travel as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to show that the program changed how people travel; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a community garden, a student took the following notes:
• The garden has 30 plots.
• Residents grow vegetables and herbs.
• Surplus produce is donated to a food bank.

The student wants to explain how the garden benefits others. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: A food bank, which gathers and distributes donated food, is one of many organizations that serve people in need.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain how the garden benefits others as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The garden is divided into 30 separate plots, each tended by a different resident who grows their own produce.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain how the garden benefits others as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Beyond its 30 plots of vegetables and herbs, the garden donates surplus produce to a local food bank.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to explain how the garden benefits others.

choices.text: Across its 30 plots, the residents of the neighborhood grow a wide variety of fresh vegetables and fragrant herbs.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain how the garden benefits others as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to explain how the garden benefits others; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a language preservation effort, a student took the following notes:
• Only 200 fluent speakers of the language remain.
• A school began teaching it to children in 2020.
• Enrollment has grown each year since.

The student wants to convey both the threat and the response. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: With only about 200 fluent speakers still living, the language is in real danger of disappearing within a generation.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not convey both the threat and the response as the prompt asks.

choices.text: With only 200 fluent speakers left, a school began teaching the language to children in 2020, and enrollment has grown every year since.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to convey both the threat and the response.

choices.text: A language, which is a shared system that lets a community communicate, can fade once its fluent speakers grow few.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not convey both the threat and the response as the prompt asks.

choices.text: In 2020 a school began teaching the language to children, and the number enrolling in those classes has grown each year.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not convey both the threat and the response as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to convey both the threat and the response; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a tornado warning system, a student took the following notes:
• New radar detects rotation earlier.
• Alerts now arrive minutes sooner.
• Earlier warnings save lives.

The student wants to stress the life-saving value of earlier alerts. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Thanks to the upgraded system, the warnings now reach people in the storm's path several minutes ahead of before.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not stress the life-saving value of earlier alerts as the prompt asks.

choices.text: By detecting rotation earlier and sending alerts minutes sooner, the new radar gives families precious extra time that saves lives.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to stress the life-saving value of earlier alerts.

choices.text: The recently installed radar is able to pick up the rotation inside a storm earlier than the older equipment could.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not stress the life-saving value of earlier alerts as the prompt asks.

choices.text: A tornado, a violently spinning column of air, can flatten buildings within seconds of touching down on the ground.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not stress the life-saving value of earlier alerts as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to stress the life-saving value of earlier alerts; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a solar farm, a student took the following notes:
• The farm covers 50 acres.
• It powers about 9,000 homes.
• It replaced a closed coal plant.

The student wants to stress the shift from fossil fuels. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Stretching across 50 acres, the solar farm is large enough to supply electricity to roughly 9,000 nearby homes.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not stress the shift from fossil fuels as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Solar farms such as this one generate electricity by capturing sunlight with rows of panels spread across the ground.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not stress the shift from fossil fuels as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The solar farm, which now occupies 50 acres of open land, delivers enough power for about 9,000 households in the area.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not stress the shift from fossil fuels as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Built on the site of a closed coal plant, the 50-acre solar farm now powers about 9,000 homes.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to stress the shift from fossil fuels.

explanation: The goal is to stress the shift from fossil fuels; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a deep-sea expedition, a student took the following notes:
• A submersible reached 6,000 meters down.
• It filmed creatures never seen before.
• Crushing pressure required a titanium hull.

The student wants to emphasize the difficulty of the conditions. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: A submersible, a small craft designed for underwater travel, can carry researchers far deeper than ordinary divers go.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the difficulty of the conditions as the prompt asks.

choices.text: During the dive, the expedition's cameras captured footage of several deep-sea creatures never before seen by anyone.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the difficulty of the conditions as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The submersible used on the expedition was built with a strong titanium hull to keep its crew safe below the surface.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the difficulty of the conditions as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Withstanding crushing pressure that demanded a titanium hull, the submersible descended 6,000 meters to film creatures never seen before.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to emphasize the difficulty of the conditions.

explanation: The goal is to emphasize the difficulty of the conditions; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching an electric bus rollout, a student took the following notes:
• The agency replaced 50 diesel buses.
• The fleet now runs nearly silent.
• Riders report a smoother, cleaner ride.

The student wants to describe how the change affected riders. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Now that 50 diesel buses have been replaced, riders enjoy a fleet that runs nearly silent and gives a smoother, cleaner ride.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to describe how the change affected riders.

choices.text: An electric bus, powered by rechargeable batteries instead of fuel, produces no exhaust as it travels its route.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not describe how the change affected riders as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The new electric buses move so quietly that pedestrians sometimes fail to hear one approaching the curb.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not describe how the change affected riders as the prompt asks.

choices.text: In a major shift, the transit agency went ahead and replaced fifty of its aging diesel buses with new models.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not describe how the change affected riders as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to describe how the change affected riders; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.
